events.title: #1 No Bugs Given
events.name: Computer Science and Engineering Society

overview.description: No Bugs Given is a competitive 1v1 debugging tournament where players race to identify and fix flaws in programs faster than their opponent.

rules: Participants face knockout rounds and must debug programs for syntax and logic errors
Strict time limit, with no AI tools permitted

events.title: CSES Arcade
events.name: Computer Science and Engineering Society

overview.description: An intense 3D, two-player duel built on suspense, strategy, and quick thinking, enhanced by immersive visuals and sound design.

rules: Players take alternating turns with randomized live or dud shells.
The round continues until only one player remains.

events.title: Mind Domino
events.name: Mathematical Society

overview.description: A multi-round contest of logic, speed, and strategy. From spotting errors to solving chained puzzles and a rapidfire finale, the sharpest minds rise to the top.

rules: Teams must attempt all rounds in order.
Each correct answer unlocks the next stage.

events.title: Math Relay Race
events.name: Mathematical Society

overview.description: The Math Relay Race is a three round challenge that blends speed, logic, and problem-solving. Presents deeper, conceptual math problems that demand clarity and reasoning.

rules: Only one final submission per team allowed.
In case of a tie, fastest total completion time wins.

events.title: TRECO HUNT : Time travel through mathematics
events.name: Mathematical Society

overview.main_title: TRECO HUNT : Time travel through mathematics
overview.description: Gear up for Treco Hunt – a thrilling race of riddles, quizzes, and hidden clues across 5 exciting rounds! Solve, unlock, and decode your way to the final treasure before anyone else!

rules: Teams must complete each round in order to move to the next.
The first team to reach the venue with the final code wins.

events.title: AI Murder Mystery
events.name: ECE Society

overview.description: Solve a thrilling mystery using AI-bot interrogations and digital clues, connecting evidence logically to uncover the hidden storyline.

rules: Solve the mystery by analyzing clues from AI bot interrogations.
Use additional software-generated hints to uncover the case plot.

events.title: Lie Detector
events.name: ECE Society

overview.description: Answer thought-provoking questions while connected to a lie detector, revealing surprising truths and playful lies in a safe environment.

rules: Take turns honestly and respect others' privacy.
Enjoy the interactive experience of uncovering truth and deception.

events.title: ChipCraft
events.name: ECE Society

overview.description: Build creative structures, automated systems, and logical circuits in Minecraft, showcasing problem-solving and technical imagination within the time limit.

rules: Collaborate effectively and follow all in-game rules.
Showcase creativity, efficiency, and precision in your designs.

events.title: MetaRush
events.name: MMES & IIMSC

overview.description: “Meta-Rush” is an adventurous race across multiple routes with exciting checkpoints, where teams unlock clues via QR codes, complete challenges, and compete to finish first with the highest score. It’s a perfect mix of strategy, speed, and fun!

rules: Teams race through 6–8 checkpoints, unlocking clues via QR codes.
Points (+4 correct, -2 wrong, 0 dares) and time decide the winner.

events.title: MetaDart
events.name: MMES & IIMSC

overview.description: “Meta-Dart” is a fun blend of skill and knowledge where each dart throw decides your challenge. The closer you land to the center, the easier the question; miss wide, and tougher material-based questions await!

rules: Players throw a dart at a 12-section material board.
Closer to center = easier questions; farther = tougher questions.

events.title: MetaLusion
events.name: MMES & IIMSC

overview.description: “Metalusion” brings futuristic materials like self-healing compounds and smart alloys to life, showcasing their fascinating real-world applications. Visitors can explore how these innovations reshape technology.

rules: Present special materials, like self-healing materials, at the stall.
Explain their interesting applications to the audience.

events.title: AI/ML
events.name: MMES & IIMSC

overview.description: “Metalusion” brings futuristic materials like self-healing compounds and smart alloys to life, showcasing their fascinating real-world applications. Visitors can explore how these innovations reshape technology.

rules: Present special materials, like self-healing materials, at the stall.
Explain their interesting applications to the audience.

events.title: Gesture Drift
events.name: Electrical Engineering Society

overview.description: The Event is a thrilling two-player competitive race where participants control their cars exclusively through hand gestures.

rules: Two players race gesture-controlled cars on a hurdle-filled track.
The first to cross the finish line wins, using only hand gestures—no remotes allowed.

events.title: Dance O'Watt
events.name: Electrical Engineering Society

overview.description: This event is a team-based interactive dance experience.

rules: This team-based interactive dance event challenges players to follow on-screen steps in sync on a 3×3 energy-harvesting tile grid.
Each correct move boosts their score, but one mistake ends the game and locks in their final score.

events.title: OptiMystery
events.name: Electrical Engineering Society

overview.description: In this event, one must synchronize strobe lights with rotating objects to reveal and decode hidden patterns for points.

rules: Participants must adjust the strobe frequency carefully, synchronize it with the rotating object, and decode the hidden information.
The patterns may reveal numbers, letters, or even riddles. The faster you decode, the higher you score!

events.title: Track To Trivia
events.name: Mechanical Engineering Society

overview.description: The F1 Quiz includes online qualifiers, a Grand Prix round (riddles, rapid-fire, strategy, buzzer), and a Final Lap (individual round). Buzzer rules apply in some rounds.

rules: Buzzer rounds: first to press answers; wrong answers incur penalties.
No audience discussion or use of phones/internet allowed.

events.title: Mechanical Kit Assembly
events.name: Mechanical Engineering Society

overview.description: Teams are given a dismantled kit and have 45 minutes to assemble it. Points awarded by time and percentage accuracy of assembly.

rules: Points awarded based on time taken and percentage assembled accurately.
Team with maximum points declared winner.

events.title: OFF Track: RC Car Showdown
events.name: Mechanical Engineering Society

overview.description: Fun races (3 laps each) at B-312, Mechanical Department; 10:00 AM–12:00 PM & 2:00 PM–5:00 PM. Overtaking and pushing are allowed; first to finish wins. No prizes.

rules: Each race has 3 laps on a circuit with turns, intersections, and elevation changes; overtaking and pushing are allowed.
Participants complete all laps; the first to finish is the winner. No prizes awarded.

events.title: NEON Cricket
events.name: Mechanical Engineering Society

overview.description: Each team has 6 players; matches are 6 overs per innings with 1 over per bowler. Runs are awarded by walls hit and bounces. Fun competitive format with specific dismissal rules.

rules: Teams of 6 play 6-over innings.
Outs include bowled, catch, run-out, stumped, LBW, hit-wicket, and one-handed catches.

events.title: KBCE
events.name: Civil Society

overview.description: KBC – Kon Banega Civil Engineer is a fast-paced quiz competition testing participants’ knowledge in civil engineering. Teams compete through multiple rounds, leading to an exciting “Hot Seat” finale where the top scorers battle for victory.

rules: Teams compete in multiple quiz rounds to advance.
Top three teams enter the Hot Seat round to determine the final winners.

events.title: Earthquake Resistant Building
events.name: Civil Society

overview.description: Earthquake Resistant Building Model challenges teams to design and construct a 4-storey structure using limited materials that can withstand rigorous load and shake tests. It’s a test of creativity, engineering skill, and structural strength!

rules: Design and build the strongest model possible.
Models must withstand rigorous load and shake table tests.
